title: The Shatterveins
atAGlance: The Shatterveins are a broken under-mountain war maze of split galleries, flooded slag cuts, bone-dry echo shafts, and ritual sinkwells driven beneath Kharad-Vul's foundations. No route stays true for long; cave-ins, Synod excavation crews, and heat-fractured stone rewrite choke points by the week. Veteran delvers navigate by sound and mineral scent more than map lines, using waymarks like Lantern Fault, the Nine-Step Drop, and Widow's Traverse to avoid dead-fall chambers. Every advance is contested in three dimensions, and the enemy's greatest weapon is not numbers but control of timing, darkness, and panic.
sageNotes: Delvers speak of the Shatterveins as if they were alive: corridors shift, echoes lie, and yesterday's map becomes tomorrow's grave marker. Reliable waypoints are rare and bitterly contested, from Chainpost Ember-17 to the Hollow Choir Well and the narrow ore chute called Sable Sluice. The Hollow Synod wages war with abduction cells, drum-code signals, and ritual beacons that pull reinforcements through side fissures before alarms can finish ringing. Survivors remember the names that still matter below: Sergeant Merra Flintspine, who barters hard truth for extraction priority, and novice geomancer Pell Dustrune, whose tremor-sense has saved more than one doomed patrol. In these tunnels, every delay is counted in missing crews, sealed refuges, and prayers that go unanswered.
gameInformation: Run each incursion as Front Segments rather than room-by-room mapping: choose objective (rescue, sabotage, sever ritual line, recover supply cache), then track Orientation, Noise, and Time as active pressures. Failed orientation checks should shift the party into a worse tactical position (crossfire ledge, split elevation, ritual cross-route) instead of ending progress. Harvest Clock beats: 2 (missing patrol reports), 4 (abduction surge and morale drop), 6 (ritual beacon network online), 8 (mass harvest event and district-wide panic). In linked surface scenes, Breach Clock beats advance whenever players withdraw without disrupting a Synod node. Reusable complications: false lantern chains guiding toward sink pits, heat blooms that snuff nonmagical light, captive lines used as bait, and shifting rubble that isolates rear ranks. Reward decisive strikes on ritual infrastructure with concrete outcomes: delayed reinforcements, reclaimed tunnel shortcuts, recovered civilians who become informants, and one-time tactical assets from Crown engineers.
